root_motivation: The wintermute agent-namespace substrate is built and booted but inert: every Claude session is born in the initial namespace because no launch path routes through agentns-claude. Before wiring can happen, there must be a typed, testable model of where sessions are born (LaunchSite), whether each is wrapped (WrapState), and what edit would route it through the wrapper (RouteAction). christen-plan builds that shared foundation — types, trait, FakeSource, and a pure plan() function — so that christen-route/christen-cap/christen-ledger have a contract to extend.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running wintermute on an Arch Linux laptop; sees agent_session=00000000000000000000000000000000 in every session; wants to understand which launch sites are unwrapped and what edit would fix each, before any tool applies changes.

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build and cargo test succeed offline; a test asserts plan makes zero source calls (it operates only on the passed &[RawSite] + injected KernelInfo + wrapper_installed).
LaunchSite, SiteKind, WrapState, RouteAction, RoutePlan are public and serde-(de)serializable; a round-trip test covers each.
ChristenConfig::load parses config/christen.example.toml; a fixture yields expected default_budget + systemd_dir + one intent_overrides entry, and an absent file yields documented defaults.

plan classifies correctly against FakeSource fixtures: systemd site whose exec line lacks agentns-claude on a -wintermute kernel with wrapper installed => Unwrapped + Wire; site whose exec line already contains agentns-claude => Wrapped + AlreadyWrapped; shell-rc site => Advise; any site on kernel with agent_ns:false or wrapper_installed:false => Skip with documented reason.
intent_for derives /build//dream//self-review/interactive for the four canonical site ids, and intent_overrides from config wins over the derivation; both covered by tests.
christen plan --format json emits one entry per site plus the RoutePlan tallies (to_wire/advised/already/skipped); schema matches the documented RoutePlan.

christen plan exits non-zero when >=1 site is Unwrapped (wrapper installed, -wintermute kernel), zero otherwise; two integration cases driving FakeSource. christen plan | head -1 does not panic (SIGPIPE reset verified by a test that closes the read end early).

README documents the config format, the type surface, the intent_for derivation table, and the LaunchSiteSource trait so christen-detect/christen-route/christen-cap/christen-ledger have a contract to extend.

scope: Typed model of launch sites: LaunchSite, SiteKind, WrapState, RouteAction, RoutePlan
Pure plan() function: &[RawSite] + KernelInfo + wrapper_installed -> RoutePlan
ChristenConfig loading from TOML with defaults
intent_for() derivation table with config override support
christen plan subcommand: table and --format json output
Exit non-zero on unwrapped sites (wintermute kernel + wrapper installed)
non_goals: Does not apply any changes to launch sites (that is christen-route)
Does not read /proc for live session data (that is christen-detect)
Does not set capabilities on binaries (that is christen-cap)
Does not parse systemd unit files from disk (that is christen-route's SystemdSource)
Does not manage audit logs or session counters (that is christen-ledger)
Does not interact with agorabus or any runtime daemon

additional.no_let_chains: MSRV 1.85 — let chains require 1.88; use if-let ladders or match instead
additional.sigpipe_reset: sigpipe::reset() must be first call in main() per self_sigpipe_panic_toolkit

five_whys_trace.q: Why do you need a launch-site model?
five_whys_trace.a: Because agent_session is always 00000000 — sessions never enter the agent namespace — and the first step to fix that is knowing which launch sites are unwrapped.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does identifying unwrapped sites require a separate crate?
five_whys_trace.a: Because christen-route, christen-cap, christen-detect, and christen-ledger all need the same LaunchSite/WrapState/RouteAction types; a shared corpus crate is the prerequisite so they don't duplicate the model.

Because purity makes the classification logic independently testable via FakeSource without touching the filesystem, and ensures christen-route/christen-cap can call plan() safely before applying any changes.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should christen-plan also include a real SystemdSource that reads ~/.config/systemd/user/?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. SystemdSource belongs in christen-route. christen-plan ships only FakeSource for tests. The trait LaunchSiteSource provides the contract.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should plan() read /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease to detect -wintermute kernel?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. KernelInfo is injected as a parameter. The caller (main) reads the OS release; plan() stays pure.
